QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_16

question.text: Sovereign Gold Bonds issued by the Government of India earn an annual interest of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sovereign Gold Bonds carry a fixed interest of 2.5% per annum on the initial investment amount, payable semi-annually. This is in addition to any appreciation in the price of gold. **The Trap:** The 2.5% is over and above the gold price appreciation — it is not instead of it. This makes SGBs superior to physical gold for investment. **Cross-Exam Context:** SGBs are covered in Series V-A (investment products) and XVII. Note: SGBs have been discontinued from FY 2024-25. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While SGBs are no longer being issued, existing bonds continue until maturity. Advise clients with existing SGBs to hold till maturity for tax-free capital gains.
